name: New York
blurb: New York operates on Eastern Time (ET), which is UTC-5 in standard time and UTC-4 during daylight saving time. As the financial capital of the world, New York time is the reference point for Wall Street trading hours, major media broadcasts, and business deadlines across the United States. The city never truly sleeps — at any hour, there are people working, commuting, and transacting across all five boroughs.
popularWith: remote workers collaborating with US East Coast teams, finance professionals, media companies, and international businesses with US headquarters

name: London
blurb: London operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) in winter and British Summer Time (BST, UTC+1) during daylight saving time. As the home of the original prime meridian, London time is a foundational reference for global timekeeping. The city is the financial hub of Europe, and the overlap between London trading hours and both New York and Asian markets makes it a critical timezone for global commerce. The London Stock Exchange opens at 8:00 AM GMT.
popularWith: international finance teams, remote workers bridging US and European time zones, software developers at multinational companies, and travelers planning European trips

name: Tokyo
blurb: Tokyo operates on Japan Standard Time (JST), which is UTC+9. Japan does not observe daylight saving time, so the offset stays constant year-round — making scheduling across this timezone unusually predictable. Tokyo is one of the world's most punctual cities; trains run to the minute and meetings start on time. The Tokyo Stock Exchange opens at 9:00 AM JST, nine hours ahead of London and fourteen hours ahead of New York.
popularWith: remote teams in the US and Australia collaborating with Japanese partners, gaming communities following Japanese game releases, and anime and tech enthusiasts tracking Tokyo-based companies

name: Los Angeles
blurb: Los Angeles operates on Pacific Time (PT), which is UTC-8 in standard time and UTC-7 during daylight saving time. LA is the entertainment capital of the world — film studios, streaming companies, and music labels all operate on Pacific Time, making it a reference point for content release schedules, streaming premieres, and Hollywood deal-making. It sits three hours behind New York, which frequently creates communication friction for US coast-to-coast teams.
popularWith: entertainment industry professionals, tech workers at West Coast companies, remote workers syncing with Silicon Valley, and international teams that need to reach US West Coast offices

name: Paris
blurb: Paris operates on Central European Time (CET, UTC+1) in winter and Central European Summer Time (CEST, UTC+2) during daylight saving time. As the cultural and political heart of France and a major European Union hub, Paris time is used across France, and closely mirrors the timezone of much of continental Europe. Paris is one hour ahead of London most of the year, and six to nine hours ahead of the United States depending on the coast.
popularWith: European business travelers, remote teams with French or EU partners, fashion industry professionals, and tourists planning Paris trips

name: Dubai
blurb: Dubai operates on Gulf Standard Time (GST), which is UTC+4. The UAE does not observe daylight saving time, making Dubai's offset permanently fixed — a significant advantage for scheduling international calls. Dubai has become a global business hub, sitting conveniently between European and Asian time zones. Its working week runs Sunday through Thursday, which is an important consideration when scheduling calls with Dubai-based partners.
popularWith: finance and logistics professionals, remote workers bridging Europe and Asia, real estate investors, and expats living or working in the UAE

name: Sydney
blurb: Sydney operates on Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST, UTC+10) and shifts to Australian Eastern Daylight Time (AEDT, UTC+11) during daylight saving time — which runs from October to April, the opposite of the Northern Hemisphere. Sydney is the financial capital of Australia and the largest city in the country. Its time zone makes early morning calls with Europe and late evening calls with the US West Coast the most common scheduling windows.
popularWith: Asia-Pacific business teams, remote workers coordinating with Australian clients, US and UK expats living in Australia, and global teams needing Pacific coverage

name: Singapore
blurb: Singapore operates on Singapore Standard Time (SGT), which is UTC+8. Singapore does not observe daylight saving time, making it a stable, predictable timezone for scheduling. As a major financial, logistics, and technology hub in Southeast Asia, Singapore time overlaps conveniently with both the Asian trading session and portions of the European business day. The Singapore Exchange (SGX) opens at 9:00 AM SGT.
popularWith: Southeast Asian business professionals, global logistics and shipping companies, fintech teams, and remote workers at Asia-Pacific offices

name: Berlin
blurb: Berlin operates on Central European Time (CET, UTC+1) and Central European Summer Time (CEST, UTC+2) during daylight saving. Germany is Europe's largest economy, and Berlin is its capital and a major tech startup hub — often called the Silicon Valley of Europe. Berlin time matches Paris, Amsterdam, Rome, and most of continental Europe, making it a natural scheduling anchor for pan-European teams.
popularWith: European remote workers, tech startup teams, software developers at German companies, and international businesses with EU operations

name: Mumbai
blurb: Mumbai operates on India Standard Time (IST), which is UTC+5:30 — a notably unusual half-hour offset that applies to the entire country. India does not observe daylight saving time. Mumbai is India's financial and entertainment capital, home to the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the massive Bollywood film industry. IST's unique offset means it sits between two common time zones, which can make it feel slightly out of step with standard international scheduling.
popularWith: outsourcing and software development teams, US and UK companies with India-based engineering teams, Bollywood followers, and the global Indian diaspora

name: Chicago
blurb: Chicago operates on Central Time (CT), which is UTC-6 in standard time and UTC-5 during daylight saving time. As the home of the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) — the world's largest futures exchange — Chicago time is deeply embedded in global commodities and derivatives trading. It sits one hour behind New York and two hours ahead of Los Angeles, making it a natural midpoint for US domestic scheduling.
popularWith: commodities traders, US Midwest businesses, remote workers on US Central Time, and teams spanning both US coasts who want a neutral scheduling reference

name: Toronto
blurb: Toronto operates on Eastern Time (ET), the same as New York — UTC-5 in standard time and UTC-4 during daylight saving time. As Canada's largest city and financial center, Toronto is home to the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) and a fast-growing tech sector. Despite sharing a timezone with New York, Toronto has a distinct business culture and calendar, with Canadian statutory holidays that differ from US federal holidays.
popularWith: US-Canada cross-border teams, financial services professionals, tech workers at Canadian companies, and remote workers in the Eastern time zone

name: Beijing
blurb: Beijing operates on China Standard Time (CST), which is UTC+8. All of China uses this single timezone, regardless of geographic span — a political decision that means sunrise in western China can occur as late as 10 AM local time. Beijing is the political capital and a major commercial hub. The Shanghai Stock Exchange (SSE) and Shenzhen Stock Exchange (SZSE) both open at 9:30 AM CST.
popularWith: international businesses with China operations, manufacturing supply chain teams, import-export professionals, and tech companies monitoring the Chinese market

name: Seoul
blurb: Seoul operates on Korea Standard Time (KST), which is UTC+9. South Korea does not observe daylight saving time. Seoul shares its UTC offset with Tokyo, making coordination between the two cities straightforward. Seoul is a global leader in technology, gaming, and K-pop culture — making KST an important reference for gaming server schedules, K-drama release times, and tech product launches from Korean companies like Samsung and LG.
popularWith: K-pop and K-drama fans, gaming communities, tech professionals at Korean companies, and remote workers in Asia-Pacific time zones

name: Amsterdam
blurb: Amsterdam operates on Central European Time (CET, UTC+1) and Central European Summer Time (CEST, UTC+2) during daylight saving time. Amsterdam is home to ASML, Booking.com, and a dense cluster of international tech companies, as well as Schiphol Airport — one of Europe's busiest transit hubs. The Amsterdam Stock Exchange (AEX) is one of the oldest in the world. Amsterdam time matches Berlin, Paris, and much of continental Europe.
popularWith: European tech workers, international logistics and travel professionals, remote teams with Netherlands-based colleagues, and finance professionals tracking the AEX

name: São Paulo
blurb: São Paulo operates on Brasília Time (BRT), which is UTC-3. Brazil abolished daylight saving time in 2019, so the offset is now fixed year-round. São Paulo is the largest city in South America and the financial heart of Brazil, home to the B3 stock exchange — one of the largest in the world by market capitalization. Its time zone sits between Eastern US time and Western European time, making it a natural bridge for transatlantic business.
popularWith: Latin American business professionals, US and European companies with Brazil operations, fintech teams, and remote workers in South America
